best_edges.edge: Leader/base run family remains the benchmark edge: 107 trades, $7125.8 net profit, 36.18 exposure hours, $196.98/exposure hour, worst MAE -527, no slow 12h trades.
best_edges.evidence: Runs 001/005/014/017 are tied leaders by robust score; latest run_018 added only $44.4 vs run_015 but degraded score, drawdown, exposure, and worst MAE badly.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze continuation is small but highly efficient and clean enough to expand carefully.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long: 7 trades, $604.5 PnL, $344.83/exposure hour, worst MAE -194.25, no slow 12h trades, median MFE 66.3 with very large selected TP distances.

best_edges.edge: Charm wall breakaway has the strongest per-trade PnL among active VALID_0DTE profiles but sample is thin.
best_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long: 5 trades, $973.1 PnL, $231.38/exposure hour, avg PnL $194.62, no slow trades, worst MAE -346.8. Prior structure tightening hurt score, so avoid quality pruning here.

best_edges.edge: Charm pin pullback is a fast, repeatable contributor despite a known tail row.
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long: 33 trades, $1216.3 PnL, $170.46/exposure hour, median hold 5.13m, avg MAE -74.3. Prior charm-pin tail-box work improved or held objective, while broad extended-VWAP pruning risks blocking 17 protected extended-VWAP winners.

best_edges.edge: 1DTE breakout fallback is profitable insurance and should not be directly tightened again.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte: 20 trades, $1300.9, $168.23/exposure hour, worst MAE -375.7, no slow trades. Two prior 1DTE quality-gate experiments reduced score by -355.7 and -594.3.

broken_edges.action: avoid expanding; revert research bias toward leader/base behavior
broken_edges.evidence: run_018 pullback re-expansion caused score -1730.97, drawdown +1.6 pts, exposure +22.38h, worst MAE from -527 to -2026.5, and one 1201m slow trade for only +$44.4 net profit.
broken_edges.problem: Pullback throughput expansion increased exposure and tail risk without improving risk-adjusted return.

broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE local quality gates cut 6-7 trades, reduced net profit by $317-$558, worsened worst MAE to -603.75, and lowered score.
broken_edges.problem: Direct 1DTE breakout quality pruning removes profitable fallback insurance faster than it improves risk.

broken_edges.action: do not revive as a profit-expansion path yet
broken_edges.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long produced zero trades in latest run despite being enabled; prior call-wall expansion hurt score by -529.7 and net profit by -$481.6.
broken_edges.problem: Enabled but currently dead/under-reachable; loosening wall acceptance has already degraded objective.

broken_edges.action: freeze stale/no-0DTE optimization except isolated robustness tests
broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE produced only 5 trades and $162.8; stale breakout had $40.5/exposure hour and avg MAE -210.6. Samples are too small, and fallback changes have not beaten leader objective.
broken_edges.problem: Small-sample fallback slice can overfit and distract from VALID_0DTE/1DTE profit expansion.

broken_edges.action: keep disabled unless separate discovery run is requested
broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long generated no trades or are disabled in the active context.
broken_edges.problem: Dormant/unused active profiles lack current evidence for profit expansion.

market_regime_read: The evidence is a favorable long-only momentum/pullback tape with 100% realized win rate, but the no-stop-loss design hides exposure quality. The leader runs are efficient: roughly $7.1k net, 36h exposure, no 12h holds, and worst MAE capped near -527. Latest run_018 is a cautionary failed profit expansion: total PnL stayed positive, but exposure ballooned to 53h, PnL/exposure fell to $116.8, drawdown rose to 2.0%, and pullback_long introduced a -2026.5 overnight tail. Profit expansion should therefore prioritize clean upside capture in already efficient profiles, not more pullback throughput.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: 1DTE breakout is profitable, but direct tightening hurt; a companion profile may separate pullback/reclaim-shaped 1DTE opportunities instead of forcing breakout rules to cover both states.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize the profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or be unreachable if profile ordering prevents activation.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries with ES pullback/reclaim state, setup quality, MAE, MFE, hold time, and profile routing attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current ~$1301, total net profit >=98% of leader, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.7, and no increase in slow trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Pullback_long appears to contain both fast winners and a deep-heat slow-to-profit state that config-only throughput expansion could not separate cleanly.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Existing config keys may not express early path failure, so a blunt split may block profitable weak-structure pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Slow-To-Profit Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-state plus early path features: first 15m MFE/MAE, half-TP touch time, target distance, structure distances, ADX slope, and option target distances.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback entries with low setup quality, weak higher-high, low target distance, and delayed profit/half-TP behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Remove or down-route the -2026.5 style tail while retaining >=90% pullback PnL and improving pullback PnL/exposure hour.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Extended-VWAP winners are real, but bad-MAE rows are near an enabled veto boundary; a model/state split could distinguish recovery-quality extension from stranded extension.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Overfit to the latest sample; extended VWAP also contains 17 protected winners worth $654.5 with median hold 1.18m.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Extended VWAP Recovery-State Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VWAP distance/slope/acceleration, rolling-high distance/minutes, structure distances, setup quality, RSI/ADX slopes, and early MFE-before-MAE path labels.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Entries above VWAP where extension is high but early progress and structure confirmation diverge.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -527 or better while retaining >=95% protected extended-VWAP winner PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant delta/charm breakout may offer profit expansion if it captures continuation not already assigned to squeeze or charm-wall breakaway.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May steal trades from stronger active profiles or remain unreachable; activation must be isolated.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Delta Charm Breakout Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router-attributed VALID_0DTE trades, overlap/cannibalization versus squeeze_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long, MAE/MFE, exposure hour contribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE continuation with strong option flow/charm context and breakout state not already captured efficiently.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 trades, positive PnL, PnL/exposure hour >200, worst MAE no worse than -300, and total score not below leader by more than small noise.

next_experiments.config_bias: Undo the failed pullback expansion bias and use the run_001/run_017 style leader as the profit baseline. Do not widen pullback_long VWAP distance or TP again until a separate tail split exists.
next_experiments.goal: Restore leader-level exposure efficiency and remove the latest -2026.5 slow-tail failure before pursuing additional profit.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The latest profit-expansion attempt was not a real edge; the leader base already dominates on risk-adjusted return.
next_experiments.name: Leader Baseline Restoration Guard
next_experiments.primary_metric: Risk-adjusted score and PnL/exposure hour, with worst MAE back near -527 and slow_12h_trades = 0.
next_experiments.risk: May give back the small +$44 raw PnL seen in run_018, but that PnL was low-quality exposure.

next_experiments.config_bias: Test modest upside capture only where the profile already shows high efficiency and contained MAE. Keep entry gates stable; focus on whether winners can hold slightly more without extending exposure materially.
next_experiments.goal: Increase PnL per trade and total PnL from the cleanest continuation profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long has enough MFE and very high PnL/exposure hour to support incremental upside capture without materially increasing MAE.
next_experiments.name: Squeeze Continuation Upside Capture V2
next_experiments.primary_metric: squeeze_long PnL and PnL/exposure hour, total score, completed trades within 10%, worst MAE not worse than -250.
next_experiments.risk: Higher targets may lengthen holds and turn a clean scalp edge into lower-quality exposure.

next_experiments.config_bias: Avoid the prior failed structure-tightening path. Instead test a very small sample-builder/target-shape experiment that preserves the broad breakaway trigger and watches cannibalization.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether charm_wall_breakaway_long can add PnL without losing its large-winner profile.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The profile’s $194.6 average PnL and $231/exposure hour are real enough to explore, but prior stricter structure gates removed value.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Breakaway Profit Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile PnL/exposure >200, profile PnL retained or expanded, total score >=98% of leader, worst MAE not worse than -400.
next_experiments.risk: Only 5 trades; a single missing winner can dominate the result.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use the enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto as a surgical diagnostic, not a broad filter. Prefer tiny threshold nudges only if the optimizer can measure protected extended-VWAP winner loss.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce deep MAE while preserving the fast extended-VWAP winners that are part of the edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst bad-MAE rows missed an already-enabled veto by narrow boundaries, but protected extended-VWAP winners make broad extension caps dangerous.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Boundary Safety Test
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, retained total PnL >=97%, protected fast/extended-VWAP winner count, and PnL/exposure hour flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Even surgical nudges may block profitable recovery winners because all latest bad-MAE trades still exited profitable.

next_experiments.config_bias: Add a router activation holdout for pullback_long_1dte only if the optimizer can isolate it from breakout_long_1dte and report cannibalization. Keep breakout_long_1dte itself unchanged.
next_experiments.goal: Find a profit-expansion path in 1DTE fallback without repeating failed breakout tightening.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Some VALID_1DTE_ONLY entries may be pullback/reclaim-shaped and deserve a companion profile rather than stricter breakout gates.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Router Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average MAE, total score, and breakout_long_1dte retained PnL.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may shift profitable breakout trades into a worse profile or produce no incremental sample.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze call_wall_acceptance_long loosening in ordinary batches. If tested, treat it as a pure sample-builder with strict pass/fail and no simultaneous changes to productive profiles.
next_experiments.goal: Determine whether the enabled but zero-trade call-wall concept is unreachable or merely too strict.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Current evidence is insufficient for profit expansion, and prior loosening hurt; a clean isolated holdout is the only useful next step.
next_experiments.name: Call Wall Acceptance Reachability Audit
next_experiments.primary_metric: At least 5 profile trades, positive PnL, average MAE better than -60, no total score degradation beyond noise.
next_experiments.risk: Loosening can steal entries from stronger pullback/squeeze/charm profiles and degrade objective as before.

research_notes: Profit expansion should be quality-first. The most recent expansion raised raw exposure, not edge: pullback_long became the dominant tail and efficiency collapsed. Do not chase trade count. The next optimizer batch should protect the leader basket, expand only high-efficiency clean profiles, and use any tail work as guardrails around extension/slow-to-profit states. Direct 1DTE tightening and call-wall loosening have already failed; any renewed work there should be split/activation research, not simple threshold tightening.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment with slow_12h_trades > 0 unless net profit and score improvement are overwhelming and repeatable.
Reject profit expansion if PnL/exposure hour falls below the leader range around $197/hour.
Keep worst MAE near leader baseline of -527; any move toward -600 or worse needs clear compensating score improvement, which past runs did not show.
Protect fast_winners, low_mae_winners, high_pnl_winners, and extended_vwap_winners; do not accept broad filters that block these groups for modest MAE cleanup.
Do not combine dormant activation with pullback re-expansion or 1DTE tightening in the same run; attribution must remain clean.
